fields.label: Sensory Manifestation Mode Source

acceptedValueNotes.modeChoice.options: Use when the sensory variants correspond to top-level modeChoice options.
acceptedValueNotes.effect: Use when the spell has one sensory manifestation directly on the effect rather than a separate mode menu.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use only when the spell has no mechanically constrained sensory manifestation.

fields.why: The runtime needs to know whether sensory variants are driven by a mode menu or by a single effect.
fields.comment: Records where the sensory variant list comes from. Purpose: Sensory Manifestation Mode Source exists so canonical prose, structured markdown, and runtime JSON do not silently disagree about whether a sound/image/etc. limit belongs to a selected spell mode or to the whole effect. Populate it from the canonical markdown prose and the structured mode data. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sensoryManifestation.modeSource`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

fields.label: Sensory Manifestation Variant Count
fields.why: Mode-based sensory spells need an explicit count so missing variants do not hide inside prose.
fields.comment: Records how many sensory manifestation variants the spell has. Purpose: Sensory Manifestation Variant Count exists so a spell with Sound and Image style branches can be checked against the runtime variant list. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].sensoryManifestation.variants.length`. The value type is `integer-or-not-applicable`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell genuinely has no such mechanic.

fields.pattern: ^Sensory Manifestation Variant \d+ (Label|Allowed Senses|Excluded Senses|Volume Range|Timing|Max Shape|Max Size|Max Size Unit)$
fields.why: Each sensory variant needs the same repeated field set so option-specific sound, sight, light, smell, timing, and size rules can be audited.
fields.comment: Numbered Sensory Manifestation Variant fields record one spell-created sensory branch at a time. Use Label for the canonical option name; Allowed Senses for what it can create; Excluded Senses for explicit negative limits; Volume Range and Timing for sound-specific rules; and Max Shape/Max Size/Max Size Unit for image or area limits. Purpose: this numbered family keeps mode-specific sensory mechanics machine-readable without creating a new top-level field for every possible option count. Runtime mapping: `effects[].sensoryManifestation.variants[]`.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal Scope

acceptedValueNotes.per_creature: Use when the reveal changes the illusion only for the creature that discerns it.
acceptedValueNotes.global: Use when the reveal changes or ends the illusion for everyone.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell has no reveal/discernment mechanic.

fields.why: Discerning an illusion may affect only the observer rather than ending the spell globally.
fields.comment: Records whether reveal is tracked per creature or globally. Purpose: Illusion Reveal Scope exists so Minor Illusion's faint-to-that-creature behavior is not confused with the spell ending. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealScope`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when the spell has no such mechanic.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal Methods

acceptedValueNotes.study_action: Use when a creature can examine the illusion with a Study action.
acceptedValueNotes.physical_interaction: Use when touching or passing through the illusion reveals it.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the spell has no reveal method.

fields.why: Different reveal methods have different runtime triggers and costs.
fields.comment: Records the methods that can reveal or discern an illusion. Purpose: Illusion Reveal Methods exists so Study checks and physical interaction are not collapsed into a generic escape check. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealRules[].method`. The value type is `csv-enum`. Use `not_applicable` when this does not apply.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal Action Cost

acceptedValueNotes.action: Use when the reveal attempt requires an action.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when the reveal method has no action cost.

fields.why: Study-based reveal uses an action, while physical interaction may be triggered by contact.
fields.comment: Records the action cost for intentional illusion examination. Purpose: Illusion Reveal Action Cost exists so Study action reveal stays tied to turn economy. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealRules[].actionCost`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when this does not apply.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal Ability

acceptedValueNotes.Intelligence: Use when the reveal check is an Intelligence check.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no ability check is involved.

fields.why: Reveal checks need the correct ability so they do not drift from canonical prose.
fields.comment: Records the ability used for a reveal check. Purpose: Illusion Reveal Ability exists so Intelligence (Investigation) reveal checks stay machine-readable. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealRules[].ability`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when this does not apply.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal Skill

acceptedValueNotes.Investigation: Use when the reveal check uses Investigation.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when no skill check is involved.

fields.why: Reveal checks need the correct skill so generic ability checks do not hide the mechanic.
fields.comment: Records the skill used for a reveal check. Purpose: Illusion Reveal Skill exists so Intelligence (Investigation) reveal checks stay machine-readable. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealRules[].skill`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when this does not apply.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal DC

acceptedValueNotes.spell_save_dc: Use when the reveal check is made against the caster's spell save DC.

fields.why: Reveal checks need a DC source, not just prose saying the creature can identify the illusion.
fields.comment: Records the DC source for a reveal check. Purpose: Illusion Reveal DC exists so Study-based reveal checks can use the same spell save DC as canonical prose. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealRules[].dc`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when this does not apply.

fields.label: Illusion Reveal Applies To
fields.why: Reveal methods can apply to some variants and not others.
fields.comment: Records which illusion or sensory variants a reveal method applies to. Purpose: Illusion Reveal Applies To exists so Study can apply to both Sound and Image while physical interaction applies only to Image. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.revealRules[].appliesTo`. The value type is `csv-string`. Use `not_applicable` only when there is no reveal method.

fields.label: Illusion Discerned State

acceptedValueNotes.faint_to_discerning_creature: Use when a discerned illusion becomes faint only to the creature that discerned it.
acceptedValueNotes.transparent_to_discerning_creature: Use when a discerned illusion becomes transparent only to the creature that discerned it.
acceptedValueNotes.not_applicable: Use when there is no post-discernment state.

fields.why: Post-reveal state can change perception without ending the illusion.
fields.comment: Records what the illusion becomes to a creature that discerns it. Purpose: Illusion Discerned State exists so faint/transparent post-reveal behavior is not treated as flavor or as a spell-ending condition. It maps to runtime JSON path `effects[].illusion.discernedState`. The value type is `enum`. Use `not_applicable` when this does not apply.
